sutta: on a certain occasion the exalted 1 was journeying along the high road between ukata and satavia now the brahman dhona was also journeying along the high road between ukata and satabia then the brahmin dhona beheld on the footprints of the exalted 1 the real mouse with a thousand spokes with their rims and hubs and all their attributes complete on seeing these he thought thus it is wonderful indeed it is marvellous indeed these will not be the footprints of 1 in human form just then the exalted 1 stepped aside from the high road and sat down at the root of a certain tree sitting cross-legged beholding holding his body upright and setting up mindfulness in front of him then the brahmin donor following up the exalted 1's footprints beheld the exalted 1 seated at the foot of a certain tree seeing him comely faith inspiring with senses calmed tranquil of mine in the attainment of composure by mastery control like a tamed alert perfectly trained elephant he approached the exalted 1 and drawing near to him said this your worship will be a deva and the buddha said no indeed brahman i am not a deva then your worship will be a gandaba no indeed brahman i am not a gandaba indeed brahman not ayaka then your worship will be a human being no indeed brahman i'm not a human being and brahman said when questioned thus your worship will be a deva a gandaba a yaka a human being you reply not so brahmin i'm not a deva a gandaba a yaka i'm not a human being who then pray will your worship be and the buddha said brahman those are savas whereby if they were not abandoned i should become a deva those asavas in me are abandoned cut off at the root made like a palm tree stump made non-existent of a nature not to arise again in future time those are savas whereby if they were not abandoned i should become a gandaba a yaka a human being those asavas in me are abandoned not to arise again in future time just as brahmin a lotus in blue red or white no born in the water grown up in the water when it reaches the surface stands there and soil by the water just so brahmin though born in the world grown up in the world having overcome the world i abide unsoiled by the world take it that i am a buddha brahmin this is 1 of those well-known sutas where this man because of seeing the footprints of the buddha these brahmins they like to observe all these marks of the human body you know and from observing the footprints of the buddha he said there must be something special about this person so he asked the buddha whether he was a deva a heavenly being and whether he was a gandaba a tree spirit a yaka another type of spiritual being a human being etc and the buddha said he was not any 1 of those but the buddha meant is that the buddha has no more ego no more self and because he has no more self he does not see himself as a human being he sees all beings as 1 with him and in fact he sees the whole world now like a dream he's mentioned somewhere else buddha comes from the word buddhi buddhi means knowledge or wisdom so the buddha is an enlightened 1 no more self no more ego now we are on the chapter of the force uh sudan number
